---
title: quarto features
subtitle: Using this for shortcodes
output: html
knitr:
  opts_chunk:
    collapse: true
    comment: '#>'
bibliography: jss.bib
---

## Citations

-   Citation: @JSSv059i10

## Code annotations

``` r
library(tidyverse)
library(palmerpenguins)
penguins |>                                      # <1>
  mutate(                                        # <2>
    bill_ratio = bill_depth_mm / bill_length_mm, # <2>
    bill_area  = bill_depth_mm * bill_length_mm  # <2>
  )                                              # <2>
```

1.  Take `penguins`, and then,
2.  add new columns for the bill ratio and bill area.

## Code filename

``` {.python filename="matplotlib.py"}
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
plt.plot([1,23,2,4])
plt.show()
```

## Callout Blocks

```{=html}
<div class="callout callout-style-default callout-note callout-titled">
<div class="callout-header d-flex align-content-center">
<div class="callout-icon-container"><i class="callout-icon"></i></div>
<div class="callout-title-container flex-fill">Note</div>
</div>
<div class="callout-body-container callout-body">
```

Note that there are five types of callouts, including: `note`,`tip`, `warning`,
`caution` and `important`.

```{=html}
</div>
</div>
```

```{=html}
<div class="callout callout-style-default callout-warning callout-titled">
<div class="callout-header d-flex align-content-center">
<div class="callout-icon-container"><i class="callout-icon"></i></div>
<div class="callout-title-container flex-fill">Warning</div>
</div>
<div class="callout-body-container callout-body">
```

Callouts provide a simple way to attract attention, for example, to this
warning.

```{=html}
</div>
</div>
```

```{=html}
<div class="callout callout-style-default callout-important callout-titled">
<div class="callout-header d-flex align-content-center">
<div class="callout-icon-container"><i class="callout-icon"></i></div>
<div class="callout-title-container flex-fill">Important</div>
</div>
<div class="callout-body-container callout-body">
```

The callout heading is provided by the callout type, with the expected heading
(i.e., Note, Warning, Important, Tip, or Caution).

```{=html}
</div>
</div>
```

```{=html}
<div class="callout callout-style-default callout-tip callout-titled">
<div class="callout-header d-flex align-content-center">
<div class="callout-icon-container"><i class="callout-icon"></i></div>
<div class="callout-title-container flex-fill">Tip With Title</div>
</div>
<div class="callout-body-container callout-body">
```

This is an example of a callout with a title. Providing a callout heading is
optional.

```{=html}
</div>
</div>
```

```{=html}
<div class="callout callout-style-default callout-caution callout-titled">
<div class="callout-header d-flex align-content-center">
<div class="callout-icon-container"><i class="callout-icon"></i></div>
<div class="callout-title-container flex-fill">Caution</div>
</div>
<div class="callout-body-container callout-body">
```

This is an example of a caution.

```{=html}
</div>
</div>
```

```{=html}
<div class="callout callout-style-simple callout-note callout-titled">
<div class="callout-header d-flex align-content-center">
<div class="callout-icon-container"><i class="callout-icon"></i></div>
<div class="callout-title-container flex-fill">Simple</div>
</div>
<div class="callout-body-container callout-body">
```

A simple callout.

```{=html}
</div>
</div>
```

```{=html}
<div class="callout callout-style-default callout-note no-icon callout-titled">
<div class="callout-header d-flex align-content-center">
<div class="callout-icon-container"><i class="callout-icon no-icon"></i></div>
<div class="callout-title-container flex-fill"><span class="screen-reader-only">Note</span>No Icon</div>
</div>
<div class="callout-body-container callout-body">
```

No icon

```{=html}
</div>
</div>
```

## Diagrams

```{mermaid}
flowchart LR
  A[Hard edge] --> B(Round edge)
  B --> C{Decision}
  C --> D[Result one]
  C --> E[Result two]
```

## HTML widgets

```{r}
#| echo: false
dir <- tempdir()
path1 <- file.path(dir, "a.txt")
writeLines(letters, path1)
path2 <- file.path(dir, "b.txt")
writeLines(letters[-(10:11)], path2)

diffviewer::visual_diff(path1, path2)
```

## Keyboard

-   Keyboard shortcut: {{< kbd Shift-Ctrl-P >}}

## Lists

-   item 2

    Continued (indent 4 spaces)

Another example

1.  ordered list

2.  item 2

    ``` python
    print("Hello, World!")
    ```

    A.  sub-sub-item 1

A task list

-   [ ] Task 1
-   [x] Task 2

A definition

term

:   definition

## Footnotes

Here is a footnote reference,[^1] and another.[^2]

[^1]: Here is the footnote.

[^2]: Here's one with multiple blocks.

    Subsequent paragraphs are indented to show that they belong to the previous
    footnote.

    ```
    { some.code }
    ```

    The whole paragraph can be indented, or just the first line. In this way,
    multi-paragraph footnotes work like multi-paragraph list items.

This paragraph won't be part of the note, because it isn't indented.

Here is an inline note.[^3]

[^3]: Inlines notes are easier to write, since you don't have to pick an
    identifier and move down to type the note.

## Equations

inline math: $E = mc^{2}$

display math:

$$E = mc^{2}$$

## Other blocks

| Line Block
|   Spaces and newlines
|   are preserved

[This text is smallcaps]{.smallcaps}

[This text is underlined]{.underline}

[This text is highlighted]{.mark}

endash: --

emdash: ---

## Shortcodes

The subtitle of this document is as follows: {{< meta subtitle >}}

{{< placeholder 400 200 >}}

{{< lipsum 1 >}}

### A section

Here we define a plot.

::: {#a-plot}
```{r}
#| echo: false
#| label: a-cell

plot(c(1, 2, 3))
```
:::

#### Note the following plot

{{< contents a-plot >}}

### Another example

We will define some values here. [`{r} "some code"`]{#a-span}

The output of that inline code cell is {{< contents a-span >}}. Etc.

## Figures

:::: {#fig-puppies layout="[[50,50], [100]]"}
![A sketch of a pitbull puppy](pitbull.jpg){#fig-pitbull}

![A sketch of a sharpei puppy](shar-pei.jpg){#fig-shar-pei}

::: {#fig-placeholder}
{{< placeholder 1000 200>}}

This is a placeholder
:::

Cute puppies (@fig-pitbull and @fig-shar-pei) and placeholder
(@fig-placeholder).
::::

## Video

::: {#fig-cern}
{{< video https://www.youtube.com/embed/wo9vZccmqwc >}}

The video "CERN: The Journey of Discovery"
:::

In @fig-cern...

## References
